evidence.quote: without RNA/DNA, with deposited X-ray diffraction data up to at least 3 A resolution and a molecular weight of less than 2000 kDa.

map–model correlation of at least 0.7, number of non-H atoms between eight and 40, a total charge of -1, 0 or +1 and a mean r.m.s.d. of at least 0.2 A for bonds, 5 degrees for angle or 30 degrees for torsion angles
The filtering resulted in a set of test cases containing 2334 ligands in 1712 models.
Protein Data Bank (PDB; Berman et al., 2000)
refined against the deposited diffraction data with phenix.refine using standard restraints.
refined with ten macrocycles of phenix.refine.

For resolutions worse than 1.5 A, all atoms were refined with isotropic ADPs. For resolutions between 1.5 and 1.2 A, protein atoms were refined as anisotropic and other 'heavy' atoms... were refined as isotropic. For resolutions better than 1.2 A, all non-H atoms were refined with anisotropic ADPs.
phenix.refine (Afonine et al., 2012)... GeoStd database (N. W. Moriarty & P. D. Adams, manuscript in preparation)
within a cutoff radius around the ligand. The default radius parameter is 3.5 A
Geometry minimization of the ligand cluster is by default performed with the PM7 method of MOPAC
terminates these with charge-neutral moieties by default
refined the models again, this time using QMR restraints... Otherwise, the strategy was the same.
As for the benchmark refinements, we extracted the minimum, maximum and mean r.m.s.d. values of bond lengths, angles and torsions for each ligand.

step_scores.label: fully_supported

evidence.quote: high-resolution bin covering better than 1.4 A... medium-resolution bins covering 1.4–2 A and the medium- to low-resolution bins covering 2–3 A
The highest resolution bin is 0.8–1.4 A
performed a t-test and computed p-values
The usage of QMR restraints therefore leads to lower valence-angle and torsion-angle r.m.s.d. values overall.
